Guideline for Developing a Code 
of Practice for Confined Space

Introduction 
Part 5 of Alberta’s Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) Code 
requires an e
mployer to have a written Code of Practice for the 
procedures to be followed when a worker enters a confined space.

ed spaces are similar and have similar hazards, but a specific 
separate permit must be issued for each confined space entry. An 
example of an entry permit is shown in Appendix 3.

answer: permit, compliant with th e requirements of this COP, is required for all confined space entries within the scope of this COP. The permit shall be prepared by a competent worker who is experienced in the work to be completed and is familiar with the hazards of the confined space. All U of C employees will use the U of C Confined Space Entry Permit in Appendix A.
